  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The contribution claim is diffuse — the calibration-protocol framing competes with the detection-accuracy finding as the core thesis; 'make this stronger' plausibly demands clarifying and sharpening which claim is load-bearing.",
    "R1.2": "Significant logical gaps undermine the argument: GPT-3 is labeled 'current frontier' and 'state of the art' while the pilot study is dated late 2024, creating an internal inconsistency; the generalizability claim ('our calibration protocol generalizes to the systems readers will most likely deploy') is asserted without warrant given GPT-3's known obsolescence relative to deployed systems.",
    "R1.3": "The inferential leap from 51.4%→58.1% accuracy to 'human evaluation of frontier-model outputs is recoverable' is stated rather than argued; the evidence partially supports the claim but the magnitude and scope of the conclusion exceed what the numbers alone establish.",
    "R1.6": "Several passages are hedged or padded in ways that weaken rhetorical force ('a modest but statistically significant improvement,' 'we argue this suggests'); tightening prose is a natural component of any 'make this stronger' instruction applied to academic writing."

  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "By 'stronger,' do you mean primarily (A) sharpening the logical arguments, evidentiary claims, and conceptual framing, or (B) improving sentence-level prose quality and rhetorical impact?"
}

rationale.R1.1: The contribution claim is diffuse — the calibration-protocol framing competes with the detection-accuracy finding as the core thesis; 'make this stronger' plausibly demands clarifying and sharpening which claim is load-bearing.
rationale.R1.2: Significant logical gaps undermine the argument: GPT-3 is labeled 'current frontier' and 'state of the art' while the pilot study is dated late 2024, creating an internal inconsistency; the generalizability claim ('our calibration protocol generalizes to the systems readers will most likely deploy') is asserted without warrant given GPT-3's known obsolescence relative to deployed systems.
rationale.R1.3: The inferential leap from 51.4%→58.1% accuracy to 'human evaluation of frontier-model outputs is recoverable' is stated rather than argued; the evidence partially supports the claim but the magnitude and scope of the conclusion exceed what the numbers alone establish.
rationale.R1.6: Several passages are hedged or padded in ways that weaken rhetorical force ('a modest but statistically significant improvement,' 'we argue this suggests'); tightening prose is a natural component of any 'make this stronger' instruction applied to academic writing.

parsed.clarification_question: By 'stronger,' do you mean primarily (A) sharpening the logical arguments, evidentiary claims, and conceptual framing, or (B) improving sentence-level prose quality and rhetorical impact?
